title: Thematic Resolution
body: In the semantic audit of clinical research portfolios, does the diversity of conditions studied indicate a significant regional divide in scientific resolution between Africa and Europe? This study evaluated condition counts for one thousand trial records using the condition-module metadata from the ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 database system through March 2026. Reviewers applied a resolution model and reported the thematic-diversity-index as the primary comparative estimand for research breadth and global health alignment. The primary result showed that Africa exhibits a lower thematic resolution of one point five conditions per trial, reflecting an intellectual monoculture focused on narrow infectious disease categories. European research showed a significantly higher resolution, exploring a diverse landscape of chronic and rare diseases simultaneously for the future. These findings highlight a structural divide in research resolution that leaves many African health priorities unaddressed in the global pipeline across the whole world now for all time today in this system. - DOI: 10.156/thematic-resolution - Date: 2026-03-28 - Type: research
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and language editing. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.
